summary: Santana‑boosted jams with soaring Morning Dew and tight Scarlet>Fire
blurb: The show shines with a guest‑driven Iko Iko → All Along the Watchtower sequence and a blistering second set that builds through Scarlet Begonias, Fire On The Mountain, Playing In The Band and culminates in an emotional Morning Dew. Jerry’s guitar is on fire early, Brent’s keyboards lift the mix, and the rhythm section drives the jams.
review: On August 23, 1987 at Calaveras County Fairgrounds the Dead delivered a memorable night highlighted by Carlos Santana’s surprise cameo on the Iko Iko → All Along the Watchtower medley. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s “perfect” tone matching Santana and a fiery solo that makes this moment one of the show’s high points. The first set opens with a warm Box of Rain, then rolls through classics like Jack Straw and West L.A. Fadeaway before the Santana‑enhanced climax. The second set is a showcase of tight transitions: Scarlet Begonias segues into Fire On The Mountain, which flows right into a powerful Playing In The Band → Drums → Space → I Need A Miracle → Dear Mr. Fantasy → Playing In The Band → Morning Dew sequence that builds to an emotional finale. Brent’s keyboards are described as “solid” and “jazzy,” especially on the second‑set jams, while Mickey and Bill provide eclectic post‑drum improvisations that keep the energy high. Though a few reviewers note a dip in Jerry’s energy later in the night, the overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, making this a must‑listen performance for anyone who loves late‑’80s Dead with a special guest twist.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across seven of nine recordings, with repeated praise for the Santana‑enhanced Watchtower, standout Box of Rain opener, tight Scarlet>Fire>Playin sequence and soaring Morning Dew. No consistent negative comments about performance; only isolated mixed notes that do not outweigh the overall enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Excellent FM‑recorded audience tape with crystal‑clear sound that captures the Santana guest and standout jams without dropouts.

key_highlights: Santana’s surprise cameo on Iko Iko → All Along the Watchtower creates an electrifying moment.
Box of Rain opens the show with a relaxed vibe that sets the tone for the night.
Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain > Playing In The Band sequence showcases tight transitions and dynamic drumming.
Morning Dew finale delivers an emotional, soaring close to the second set.

band_performance.Jerry: Confident and gutsy on guitar, matching Santana’s fire on Watchtower and delivering a blistering solo early; energy wanes slightly later but overall performance is strong.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with strong vocal work on Box of Rain and consistent phrasing throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Steady rhythm guitar that holds his own, especially in the early blues numbers where he even outshines Jerry according to some listeners.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards shine with solid electric‑piano work, jazzy B3 pads, and a pivotal role on Playing In The Band; lifts the second‑set mix despite one reviewer missing him on Mr. Fantasy.
band_performance.Mickey: Dynamic drumming that fuels the jams, providing eclectic post‑drum improvisations and driving the energy into Space.
band_performance.Billy: Energetic interplay with Mickey, delivering concise drum breaks and contributing to the powerful rhythm section throughout.
